title: 5:6-BENZOQUINALDINIC ACID AS AN ANALYTICAL REAGENT. PART III. ESTIMATION OF ZINC, COBALT, NICKEL AND MANGANESE
author: ANIL KUMAR MAJUMDAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The reagent 5 : 6-benzoquinaldinic acid has been used for the estimation of zinc. cobalt, nickel and manganese. The points or incipient precipitation for these elements are at about  P  H  2.08, 2.14, 2.25 and 1.75 respectively and the respective  P  H  regions for their complete precipitation are at and over 2.85, 3.24, 3.00 and 2.90. The salt may be dried at 110º -115º and weighed as the hydrated salts, e.g., zinc, with one mole of water, cobalt with two, and both nickel and manganese with two and a half moles of water. The cobalt salt may also be dried at 150º-155º and wieghed as the anhydrous salt.
steps_raw: The reagent 5 : 6-benzoquinaldinic acid has been used for the estimation of zinc. cobalt, nickel and manganese. The points or incipient precipitation for these elements are at about  P  H  2.08, 2.14, 2.25 and 1.75 respectively and the respective  P  H  regions for their complete precipitation are at and over 2.85, 3.24, 3.00 and 2.90. The salt may be dried at 110º -115º and weighed as the hydrated salts, e.g., zinc, with one mole of water, cobalt with two, and both nickel and manganese with two and a half moles of water. The cobalt salt may also be dried at 150º-155º and wieghed as the anhydrous salt.
